description: Page-level UI redesign: apply Anthropic brand system (from prd_16) to every page — landing, auth, dashboard, calendar, analytics, settings, job board, modals, and empty states. Depends on prd_16 design tokens being merged first.

userStories.title: Redesign landing page with editorial Anthropic aesthetic
userStories.description: Rewrite frontend/src/pages/LandingPage.jsx to match Anthropic's warm editorial style. Hero section: bg-surface-secondary (#faf9f5), large Poppins heading (text-4xl md:text-5xl font-display font-semibold tracking-tight text-gray-900), Lora body paragraph (text-lg text-gray-500 max-w-2xl leading-relaxed), single solid clay CTA button (BUTTON_PRIMARY token). Remove any gradient hero backgrounds, decorative blobs, or animated illustrations. Features section: 3-column grid of CARD_BASE cards, each with a simple icon (single-color gray-400, no colored circles), Poppins heading, Lora description. Footer: minimal, bg-white border-t border-border-default, warm gray text. Overall: generous whitespace (py-24 between sections), no visual clutter, content-first. The page should feel like reading a well-designed editorial site, not a SaaS template.
acceptanceCriteria: Hero uses bg-surface-secondary, not gradient or colored background
Heading uses font-display (Poppins), body uses font-sans (Lora)
CTA button uses BUTTON_PRIMARY token (solid clay, no gradient)
Feature cards use CARD_BASE token (border, no shadow)
Icons are single-color gray, not colored circles
Sections have py-24 spacing minimum
No decorative blobs, gradients, or animated illustrations
npm run build succeeds
userStories.notes: Landing page is the first impression. Must feel warm, editorial, confident — not flashy SaaS.

userStories.title: Redesign login and register pages
userStories.description: Update frontend/src/pages/Login.jsx and frontend/src/pages/Register.jsx. Layout: centered card on bg-surface-secondary, max-w-sm. Card: CARD_BASE token, px-8 py-10. Brand mark at top: text wordmark 'Pipelined' in font-display font-semibold text-xl text-gray-900 (no logo image). Heading: font-display text-xl font-semibold text-gray-900. Inputs use INPUT_BASE token with INPUT_LABEL above each. Submit button full-width BUTTON_PRIMARY. Social login buttons (GitHub) use BUTTON_SECONDARY. Link text (forgot password, switch to register): text-brand-500 hover:text-brand-600 text-sm. Remove any decorative side panels, background patterns, or illustration art. The auth pages should be clean, minimal, and fast to scan. Also update ForgotPassword.jsx and ResetPassword.jsx to match the same card layout.
acceptanceCriteria: Login/Register use centered card on bg-surface-secondary
Brand wordmark in Poppins, no logo image
All inputs use INPUT_BASE and INPUT_LABEL tokens
Submit buttons use BUTTON_PRIMARY (solid clay)
GitHub OAuth button uses BUTTON_SECONDARY
No decorative panels, patterns, or illustrations
ForgotPassword and ResetPassword pages match the same layout
npm run build succeeds
userStories.notes: Auth pages are the entry point. Clean and trustworthy > flashy.

userStories.title: Redesign dashboard layout and navigation
userStories.description: Update the Dashboard page (frontend/src/pages/Dashboard.jsx) and the main navigation component. Nav: use NAV_CONTAINER token, horizontal top bar with NAV_BRAND on left, NAV_LINK items (Dashboard, Calendar, Analytics, Jobs, Activity), user menu on right. Active link uses NAV_LINK_ACTIVE. Remove any colored sidebar or gradient nav backgrounds. Dashboard content area: bg-surface-secondary with max-w-7xl mx-auto px-6. Stats bar at top: use CARD_BASE cards in a grid, each showing a number in font-display text-2xl font-semibold and a label in text-sm text-gray-500. Remove any colored stat icons or gradient stat cards. Pipeline columns/kanban: CARD_BASE for each column, application cards inside use border-border-default bg-white hover:border-border-strong. Status badges use the BADGE_* tokens from US-223. Overall: clean grid layout, generous spacing, no visual noise.
acceptanceCriteria: Top nav uses NAV_CONTAINER, NAV_LINK, NAV_LINK_ACTIVE tokens
No colored sidebar or gradient nav background
Stats cards use CARD_BASE with font-display numbers
Pipeline cards use border-based depth, not shadows
Status badges use BADGE_* tokens
Content area is max-w-7xl with generous padding
npm run build succeeds
userStories.notes: Dashboard is the most-used page. Must feel organized and calm, not cluttered.

userStories.title: Redesign calendar page
userStories.description: Update frontend/src/pages/Calendar.jsx. Page header: font-display text-xl font-semibold, month navigation with BUTTON_GHOST arrows. Calendar grid: bg-white border border-border-default rounded-xl. Day cells: border-border-default dividers, today highlighted with bg-brand-500/10 (subtle warm tint, not solid color). Event pills: rounded-full px-2 py-0.5 text-xs, using muted status colors (BADGE_* palette — not saturated). Hover on event shows tooltip with CARD_BASE styling. Month/week toggle: BUTTON_SECONDARY group. Remove any colored day headers or gradient event backgrounds.
acceptanceCriteria: Calendar container uses border-border-default, not shadow
Today cell uses subtle bg-brand-500/10 highlight
Event pills use muted BADGE_* colors, not saturated fills
Navigation buttons use BUTTON_GHOST
Font-display for page heading and day-of-week headers
npm run build succeeds
userStories.notes: Calendar is data-dense — warm borders and muted colors prevent visual overload.

userStories.title: Redesign analytics page
userStories.description: Update frontend/src/pages/Analytics.jsx. Page header: font-display text-xl. Chart containers: CARD_BASE with generous p-6 padding. Chart color palette: use the three Anthropic accent colors as the primary series — #d97757 (orange), #6a9bcc (blue), #788c5d (green), plus gray-300 and gray-400 for secondary series. Remove any indigo/violet chart colors. Axis labels: text-xs text-gray-400 font-sans (Lora). Chart tooltips: bg-white border border-border-default rounded-lg shadow-card text-sm. Summary stat cards at top: same style as dashboard stats (font-display numbers, text-gray-500 labels). Remove any gradient chart backgrounds or colored card headers.
acceptanceCriteria: Chart series use #d97757, #6a9bcc, #788c5d as primary colors
No indigo/violet in any chart color
Chart containers use CARD_BASE token
Axis labels use text-gray-400
Tooltips use warm border styling
npm run build succeeds
userStories.notes: Charts need the brand accent trio to feel cohesive with the rest of the UI.

userStories.title: Redesign job board page
userStories.description: Update frontend/src/pages/JobBoard.jsx. Page header: font-display text-xl, search/filter bar with INPUT_BASE styling. Job cards: CARD_BASE, company name in font-display font-medium text-gray-900, job title in font-sans text-gray-700, location/salary in text-sm text-gray-400. Tags (remote, full-time, etc.) use TAG token. Save/apply button: BUTTON_PRIMARY (small variant: px-3 py-1.5 text-xs). Card hover: border-border-strong transition. Remove any colored company logo placeholders or gradient card accents. Grid layout: responsive 1-2-3 columns with gap-4.
acceptanceCriteria: Job cards use CARD_BASE with border hover
Company name in font-display, details in font-sans
Tags use TAG token (rounded-full, surface-secondary bg)
Apply button uses BUTTON_PRIMARY
No colored logo placeholders or gradient accents
Responsive grid layout
npm run build succeeds
userStories.notes: Job board has many cards — clean layout with warm borders prevents visual fatigue.

userStories.title: Redesign settings page
userStories.description: Update frontend/src/pages/Settings.jsx. Layout: max-w-2xl mx-auto on bg-surface-secondary. Section cards: CARD_BASE with font-display section headings (text-lg font-semibold) and font-sans body/descriptions. Form inputs use INPUT_BASE with INPUT_LABEL. Save buttons: BUTTON_PRIMARY. Danger zone (delete account): border-red-200 bg-red-50 card variant with BUTTON_DANGER. Tab/section navigation: text links with NAV_LINK_ACTIVE styling for current section. Remove any colored section headers or icon-heavy layouts.
acceptanceCriteria: Settings layout is max-w-2xl centered
Section cards use CARD_BASE
Headings in font-display, body in font-sans
Inputs use INPUT_BASE + INPUT_LABEL tokens
Danger zone uses red border/bg variant
npm run build succeeds
userStories.notes: Settings is a form-heavy page — clean typography hierarchy matters most here.

userStories.title: Redesign all modal dialogs
userStories.description: Update all modal components across the app to use the new tokens. Every modal: MODAL_BACKDROP (bg-black/30 backdrop-blur-sm) + MODAL_CARD (bg-white rounded-2xl border border-border-default shadow-lg). Modal header: font-display text-lg font-semibold text-gray-900, with a BUTTON_GHOST close button (X icon in gray-400). Modal body: font-sans text-gray-700. Modal footer: border-t border-border-default pt-4, buttons right-aligned. This includes: UpgradePlanModal, CommandPalette, ShortcutHelp, any confirmation dialogs, application detail modals, and the feedback widget. All modals should have consistent spacing: p-6 body, gap-4 between sections.
acceptanceCriteria: All modals use MODAL_BACKDROP and MODAL_CARD tokens
Modal headers use font-display, bodies use font-sans
Close button is BUTTON_GHOST with gray-400 icon
Modal footers have border-t border-border-default
Consistent p-6 padding across all modals
npm run build succeeds
userStories.notes: Modals appear in every flow — consistency here ties the whole redesign together.

userStories.title: Redesign empty states and loading states
userStories.description: Update all empty state illustrations and loading indicators. Empty states (no applications, no events, no contacts): centered layout with a simple line icon (gray-300, 48px), font-display heading ('No applications yet'), font-sans description in text-gray-500, single BUTTON_PRIMARY CTA. Remove any colorful illustrations, gradient backgrounds, or oversized icons. Loading spinner: replace the current blue-500 spinner with brand-500 (clay) border, thinner border-2 (from border-4), slightly larger h-6 w-6. Skeleton loading: use bg-surface-tertiary (#e8e6dc) shimmer instead of gray-200. These states should feel intentional and warm, not like something broke.
acceptanceCriteria: Empty states use line icons in gray-300, not colorful illustrations
Empty state headings in font-display, descriptions in font-sans
Loading spinner uses brand-500 color and border-2
Skeleton shimmer uses bg-surface-tertiary
No colorful illustrations or gradient empty state backgrounds
npm run build succeeds
userStories.notes: Empty/loading states are seen frequently — they shape the perceived quality of the app.

userStories.title: Replace all icon styling to single-color warm gray
userStories.description: Audit all Lucide icon usage across the frontend (grep for lucide-react imports). Currently icons are wrapped in colored circles (bg-indigo-100 text-indigo-600, bg-blue-100 text-blue-600, etc.) or use brand-colored fills. Replace all icon styling: remove colored circle wrappers, icons should be bare with text-gray-400 (default), text-gray-600 (emphasis), or text-brand-500 (interactive/accent). Icon size should be consistent: w-5 h-5 for inline, w-6 h-6 for standalone, w-8 h-8 for empty states. No colored backgrounds behind icons — Anthropic uses bare monochrome icons. Exception: status-specific icons (checkmark for success, X for error) can use their BADGE_* token text color.
acceptanceCriteria: No bg-*-100 text-*-600 colored circle icon wrappers remain
Default icons are text-gray-400 or text-gray-600
Interactive icons use text-brand-500
Icon sizes are consistent (w-5/w-6/w-8 based on context)
Status icons use matching BADGE_* text colors
npm run build succeeds
userStories.notes: Colored circle icons are the #1 'AI-generated' tell. Bare monochrome icons are the fastest visual upgrade.

userStories.title: Remove all gradient usage across the frontend
userStories.description: Search the entire frontend/src directory for gradient classes: bg-gradient-to-*, from-*, to-*, via-*. Remove every instance and replace with solid colors using the new token system. Common replacements: gradient buttons become BUTTON_PRIMARY (solid bg-brand-500), gradient hero backgrounds become bg-surface-secondary, gradient card accents become border-brand-500, gradient text becomes text-brand-500. Also check for any inline style gradients. After this story, zero CSS gradient classes should exist in the frontend codebase (except in any third-party CSS that can't be changed).
acceptanceCriteria: grep -r 'bg-gradient\|from-\|to-\|via-' frontend/src returns zero matches (excluding node_modules)
All former gradients replaced with solid brand colors or neutral backgrounds
No inline style gradients in JSX
Visual appearance is consistent — no missing backgrounds or invisible text
npm run build succeeds
userStories.notes: Gradients are the #2 'AI-generated' tell after colored icon circles. Must be fully purged.
